subject: Mozi Minute: Expensive Leads Fix
body:
Words I like: Your leads rarely cost too much, you make too little from them. But if they really do cost too much, there are 6 fixes.

Mozi Minute: Expensive Leads Fix

If your leads cost more than industry benchmarks…you're profitable, but barely.

Most businesses panic and cut ad spend or chase cheaper platforms.

Wrong move.

Here's the hierarchy of fixes that actually work:

Fix #1: Better Offer (Fastest ROI).

Faster, Do more for them, Remove more risk - or all three.

And when in doubt - deliver faster. Test it for one week.

Even a modest increase in CTRs/lead conversion can drop lead cost significantly. No new traffic needed.

Fix #2: CRO Your Funnel (Medium effort, high return)

Track every step: landing page to call booked to show rate to close rate. Find your biggest leak and plug it first.

Most businesses lose the majority of leads between booking and showing. Fix that before buying more leads.

Fix #3: Better Ad/Content Creative

Look at your last 100 ads or creatives.

Look at the hooks. Reuse the best 10 and make 10 pieces/ads with each of them. Rinse & repeat.

Fix #4: Nurture Your List (Long-term gold)

That lead who didn't buy today might buy in months. Build an email sequence that provides value weekly.

I've seen businesses dramatically increase revenue just by properly following up with existing leads.

Fix #5: Content Marketing if you aren't already (Slower start, compounds forever)

Start creating helpful content in your space. In the short term it'll increase your conversion on outbound and ads.

Long term, it takes time to gain traction, but eventually generates more free leads than your other avenues.

Fix #6: Referral System (Underused goldmine)

Your best customers know other people like them. Create a simple referral program. Track it.

Referrals typically close much higher than cold leads and cost significantly less.

Fix #7: Review Optimization (Free but takes work)

More reviews = higher conversion rates from the same traffic.

Get aggressive about collecting reviews. Respond to all of them. Businesses with many reviews convert much better than those with few.

The hierarchy matters.

Start with offer improvements and funnel fixes.

Those give you immediate relief.

Then build the long-term engines.

Most businesses skip straight to "find cheaper traffic" and wonder why they're stuck in a race to the bottom.

As promised, value in under a minute.

Make leads worth more, don't pay less,

Alex
